title: Nouns Related to Cinema and Theater

senses.definition: a movie, TV program, etc. that is based on a book or play

examples.x: The film adaptation of the bestselling novel stayed true to the book's plot and characters.
The adaptation of the classic Shakespearean play received critical acclaim for its innovative approach to the source material.
Hollywood has produced countless adaptations of literary works, bringing beloved stories to life on the silver screen.
The adaptation remained faithful to the original stage play, preserving its timeless themes and dialogue.
The adaptation of the Broadway musical featured elaborate sets and stunning choreography that dazzled audiences.

senses.definition: a motion picture including no colors except the colors black, white and a range of gray
a low-budget motion picture that is considered to be of low quality
a motion picture that is aimed at a female audience, usually depicting a romantic relationship
a motion picture or theatrical production with a historical setting in which the actors wear the costume appropriate to that time period

examples.x: The costume drama depicted the court intrigue of medieval Europe with stunning accuracy.
The theater company's latest production is a lavish costume drama set during the Renaissance.
Costume dramas often feature elaborate ballroom scenes with actors in period attire.
He starred in several costume dramas portraying historical figures from different epochs.
The costume drama's wardrobe department meticulously recreated 18th-century fashion for the actors.

senses.definition: a version of a motion picture that is edited in a way that the director wanted it to be originally, containing scenes that are not included in the studio version
a set of related movies or novels that portray the same character or characters in different settings and situations
a style of art, music, literature, film, etc. that has its own special features

examples.x: Impressionism is a genre of painting known for its focus on light and color.
Ballet is a classical genre of dance that emphasizes grace and technique.
Abstract art is a genre that breaks away from traditional representation.
The Renaissance period saw the emergence of the genre of portraiture.
Film noir is a genre known for its dark themes and moody visuals.

senses.definition: a story that we can watch on a screen, like a TV or in a theater, with moving pictures and sound

examples.x: The film we watched last night was a gripping thriller that kept us on the edge of our seats until the very end.
The classic film "Casablanca" is often hailed as one of the greatest romance movies of all time.
As part of their film studies course, students analyzed the cinematography and narrative structure of various iconic films.
This year's film festival showcased a diverse range of independent films from both emerging and established filmmakers around the world.

senses.definition: a French film movement in the 1960s known for its innovative techniques and fresh approach to storytelling
‌a novel, motion picture, etc. that depicts the events and stories taking place before the events of an earlier work
a motion picture or piece of music that is made based on an old song or movie
a book, movie, play, etc. that continues and extends the story of an earlier one

examples.x: The highly anticipated sequel to the bestselling novel was released to critical acclaim.
Fans eagerly lined up to see the sequel to their favorite movie on opening night.
The sequel picked up right where the first book left off, continuing the protagonist's journey with new challenges and adventures.
After the success of the first play, the theater announced plans for a sequel to further explore the characters and their relationships.
The sequel exceeded expectations, introducing new twists and revelations that kept audiences on the edge of their seats.

senses.definition: a movie, novel, play, etc. that is initially underappreciated, but gains sudden and unexpected success later on
production of something new based on a successful movie or TV show
a motion picture that is set to a soundtrack, as opposed to a silent movie
a narrative that makes the audience feel extremely sad and excessively sentimental
a movie that is intended to be broadcast on TV, rather than being projected on the screen

examples.x: She starred in a romantic telefilm that aired last weekend on a popular channel.
The telefilm received rave reviews for its gripping storyline and strong performances.
He directed a suspenseful telefilm that kept viewers on the edge of their seats.
The telefilm's production budget allowed for stunning visual effects and elaborate sets.
The telefilm's cast included well-known actors who brought depth to their roles.

senses.definition: a sad movie, play, book, etc. that is too sentimental and makes the audience cry

examples.x: The movie "The Notebook" is a classic weepy that tells the heart-wrenching love story of Noah and Allie, leaving audiences reaching for tissues.
The play "Terms of Endearment" is a weepy that spans decades in the lives of a mother and daughter, exploring the highs and lows of their relationship with emotional depth.

senses.definition: a motion picture, TV or radio program, etc. that someone has contributed to
a group of people who have gathered to watch and listen to a play, concert, etc.

examples.x: He was nervous to speak in front of a large audience.
She scanned the audience looking for her family.
The audience clapped loudly at the end of the performance.
The audience was moved by the powerful speech.
The theater was filled with an excited audience.

senses.definition: someone who frequently goes to watch plays at a theater
a comic play, movie, book, etc. that portrays the behaviors of a particular social class, satirizing them

examples.x: "Emma" by Jane Austen explores matchmaking in Regency England, a classic comedy of manners.
"Clueless" offers a modern take on the comedy of manners genre.
"An Ideal Husband" by Oscar Wilde entertains with its biting comedy of manners, critiquing Victorian moralities.
"Metropolitan" humorously portrays New York City socialites in a comedy of manners setting.
Richard Brinsley Sheridan's "The School for Scandal" satirizes 18th-century British society with sharp comedy of manners.

senses.definition: a play or movie that uses exaggerated humor, absurd situations, and improbable events to entertain

examples.x: The play was a classic farce, filled with mistaken identities and ridiculous twists.
His latest film is a political farce mocking government inefficiency.
The classic farce relied on slapstick humor and mistaken identities.
Slipping on a banana peel is a common gag in a theatrical farce.
Many comedies rely on farce to create exaggerated humor and chaos.

senses.definition: a movie, novel, play, etc. that is set in an earlier historical era
a book, painting, play, etc. that is created according to the common taste of the public in order to earn money
something that is extremely successful, such as a song, motion picture, play, etc.
a story, play, movie, etc. about a mystery or murder that the audience cannot solve until the end

examples.x: The latest novel by the bestselling author is a gripping whodunit, filled with twists and turns that keep readers guessing until the very end.
The classic Agatha Christie story is the quintessential whodunit, with a group of suspects, a clever detective, and a shocking revelation.
The film is an engaging whodunit, following the detective's meticulous investigation to uncover the identity of the elusive murderer.
At the dinner party, the guests enjoyed a live-action whodunit, participating in a murder mystery game where everyone had to solve the crime.
The TV series became a hit for its compelling whodunit plotlines, where each episode presented a new mystery for the viewers to solve.

senses.definition: someone who frequently goes to watch movies at a cinema
something that is unsuccessful or fails to meet expectations, such as a movie, play, or product
a piece of art created with great skill, which is an artist's best work

examples.x: The Mona Lisa is often regarded as Leonardo da Vinci's masterpiece, admired by millions worldwide.
After years of dedication, the artist finally unveiled what critics called his masterpiece.
The sculpture was hailed as a masterpiece, showcasing the artist's exceptional talent and vision.
She spent months perfecting her painting, hoping it would be considered her masterpiece.
The gallery's centerpiece was a masterpiece that captured the essence of human emotion.

senses.definition: products such as clothes, toys, etc. that are related to a motion picture, TV show or sports team; the process of selling these products
an article published in a newspaper or magazine that praises a movie, book, etc.

examples.x: The new Broadway show received rave reviews from critics.
Her latest novel earned rave reviews for its compelling storytelling.
The film got rave reviews and quickly became a box office hit.
After its premiere, the documentary earned rave reviews from audiences.
His performance in the play was met with rave reviews.

senses.definition: a report that is published in a newspaper or a magazine, in which someone gives an opinion of a play, movie, book, etc.

examples.x: According to the review, the new novel is a must-read.
Before going to the theater, she read a review of the play.
I checked online reviews before buying the new gadget.
I read a glowing review of the new movie in the newspaper.
The movie got mixed reviews from critics.

senses.definition: unwanted information about how the plot of a movie, game, book, etc. develops or ends that can ruin one's enjoyment

examples.x: He accidentally gave away a spoiler about the ending of the movie.
Please avoid spoilers in the review if you want to keep the plot a surprise.
Some people can’t resist sharing spoilers, even if others haven’t seen the film yet.
I avoid reading comments online before watching a show to avoid spoilers.
He asked for no spoilers when discussing the latest video game.
The film’s spoiler was so widely shared that many people didn’t bother to watch it.

senses.definition: the duration of a musical performance, theater, or motion picture
the job or art of performing in movies, plays or TV series

examples.x: Despite being a comedy, the film required some serious acting.
I've always admired her acting skills.
The movie was good, but the acting was even better.

senses.definition: the opening night at which a play, movie, etc. is presented to the public
a moment or pause in a scene where a character experiences a change in emotion or thought, often used to build tension, convey subtext, or advance the story
the music composed for a movie

examples.x: The composer received critical acclaim for his hauntingly beautiful score for the epic film.
The orchestra rehearsed tirelessly to perfect the intricate score for the upcoming ballet performance.
The director collaborated closely with the composer to ensure the score captured the mood and emotion of each scene.
The film's score won an Academy Award for Best Original Music, cementing its place in cinematic history.
The composer drew inspiration from the film's storyline to create a poignant and evocative score that resonated with audiences.

senses.definition: the first night in which a play is publicly performed or a movie is presented for public view
a story told through a series of moving pictures with sound, usually watched via television or in a cinema

examples.x: He watched a scary movie and got scared during the suspenseful scenes.
I watched a comedy movie and couldn't stop laughing.
She downloaded a new movie to watch on her laptop during the flight.
We bought popcorn and soda to enjoy during the movie.
We discussed our favorite movie scenes with our friends after watching a film.

senses.definition: the events that are crucial to the formation and continuity of a story in a movie, play, novel, etc.

examples.x: The plot of the novel kept readers on the edge of their seats with its unexpected twists.
The movie's plot was intricate, weaving together multiple storylines.
She explained the plot of the play, highlighting the main conflicts and resolutions.
The plot of the TV series unfolded gradually, revealing secrets about each character.
Critics praised the plot of the film for its originality and depth.

senses.definition: a part of a movie, play or book in which the action happens in one place or is of one particular type

examples.x: The dream scene in the movie was very surreal.
The final scene of the play was very emotional.
The opening scene of the movie was shot in Paris.
The scene in the restaurant was hilarious.
They filmed the beach scene on a cold day.

senses.definition: a written text that a movie, show, or play is based on

examples.x: The actor memorized his lines from the script before the performance.
The movie script was praised for its witty dialogue and emotional depth.
She worked with the director to revise the script before filming began.
The script for the play included detailed instructions for each scene.
He submitted his script to the studio, hoping for it to be turned into a film.
The cast received their scripts ahead of the first rehearsal.
The film's script was adapted from a popular novel.

senses.definition: the events that have happened to a character before their story in a book, movie, etc. begins

examples.x: The author revealed the protagonist's tragic backstory through a series of flashbacks scattered throughout the novel.
The movie provided a brief glimpse into the character's backstory, hinting at a troubled past that influenced their present actions.
Understanding the villain's backstory added depth to the narrative, revealing the motivations behind their sinister deeds.
The actress delved deep into her character's backstory to better portray her complex emotions on screen.
The video game's immersive storyline included optional quests that allowed players to uncover hidden aspects of the protagonist's backstory.

senses.definition: a short pause between parts of a play, movie, etc.

examples.x: The orchestra performed a lively piece during the intermission to entertain the audience.
The intermission allowed the crew to make quick changes to the set for the next scene.
Moviegoers used the intermission to stretch their legs and visit the restroom.
During the intermission, patrons enjoyed refreshments in the theater lobby.
She chatted with friends during the intermission about their favorite moments from the performance.

senses.definition: the most significant moment in a story, play, movie, etc. with a high dramatic suspense

examples.x: The climax of the novel left readers on the edge of their seats, eagerly anticipating the resolution.
She carefully crafted the climax of the play to elicit maximum emotional impact from the audience.
The film's climax featured a thrilling showdown between the hero and the antagonist, culminating in a dramatic confrontation.
The climax of the movie revealed the protagonist's true identity, unraveling a series of shocking revelations.
The climax of the play marked a turning point in the protagonist's journey, leading to a profound transformation.

senses.definition: the final part of a story, movie, etc.

examples.x: He thought the ending of the mystery novel was quite clever.
She rewrote the ending of her story to make it more impactful.
The ending of the play was very dramatic.
The TV series had an unexpected ending that surprised all viewers.

senses.definition: a short interval between parts of a play, movie, etc.

examples.x: During the interlude, the stage was rearranged for the second act.
The interlude between the film's two halves allowed viewers to discuss the plot.
During the interlude, the audience enjoyed a brief dance performance.
The interlude in the movie served as a transition to a new storyline.
The interlude gave the actors a chance to rest and change costumes.

senses.definition: a person who provides a spoken commentary for a TV show, movie, etc. whom the audience cannot see
the beginning section of a movie, book, play, etc. that introduces the work

examples.x: The prologue of the novel set the scene for the epic adventure that followed.
The play’s prologue provided crucial context for understanding the characters' motivations.
She was captivated by the prologue, which hinted at the mysteries to come in the book.
Before diving into the main narrative, the prologue gave a brief overview of the book’s setting.
In the movie's prologue, viewers were given a glimpse of the backstory that explained the plot.

senses.definition: the time and place in which the story of a movie, play, etc. is taking place

examples.x: The setting of the movie is a futuristic city in the year 2050.
The play's setting is a small village in the 19th century.
The novel's setting includes various locations in Italy during World War II.
The TV series has a modern-day setting in New York City.
The setting of the fantasy saga is an ancient kingdom filled with magic.

senses.definition: a series of events in a novel, movie, etc. that is separate from the main story and is less important but is linked to it
spoken descriptions given in a movie or a television show, etc. by a narrator that is not seen by the audience
a short part of a movie or broadcast that is viewed separately

examples.x: The director shared a clip from the film during the promotional event.
She watched a funny clip of the comedian's latest stand-up routine.
The news program included a clip of the interview with the celebrity.
He edited a clip of his favorite scenes to share on social media.

senses.definition: an act of displaying a movie or TV show
a film in which two close friends go on an adventure together
